title: Why Speculative Decoding Is Free
concept: Because verifying multiple tokens in parallel takes the same time on GPU hardware as generating one token, speculative decoding increases speed with zero loss in output quality.

exercises.prompt: What is the main goal of speculative decoding?

options.text: To make the model use less memory.
To train the model on new data.

exercises.explain: Speculative decoding is a strategy used to speed up large language models without changing their output quality.

cards.title: Speed for Free!
cards.body: What if we could speed up smart AI models by 2 to 3 times without losing any quality, and without needing bigger computers? It sounds like magic, but it is just clever GPU math!

cards.title: What is Speculative Decoding?
cards.body: Speculative decoding is a speed-up technique where a small, fast 'draft' model guesses several future words, and a large 'target' model checks them all at once. Because GPUs process batches of data in parallel, verifying five words takes the same time as generating just one word!

cards.title: The Chef and the Helper
cards.body: Imagine a master chef (the big LLM) and a fast kitchen helper (the draft model). The helper quickly guesses a list of ingredients needed for a recipe. The master chef glances at the entire list all at once and either nods 'yes' or points out where the helper made a mistake. Looking at a whole list takes the chef no more time than looking at a single ingredient!

cards.title: A Quick Correction
cards.body: Let's say we want to generate 'The cat sat on the mat'. A tiny model quickly guesses 'sat on the rug'. The big model checks all four words at once. It accepts 'sat on the', rejects 'rug', and corrects it to 'mat'. We got three correct words in the time it usually takes to get just one!

cards.title: Does Quality Drop?
cards.body: Many people think that using a smaller, less accurate model to draft words makes the final output worse. In reality, the big model verifies every single word. If the small model makes a mistake, the big model corrects it instantly. The final output is exactly the same high quality!

cards.title: One-by-One vs. All-at-Once
cards.body: Compare the traditional way of generating words one by one versus the speculative way of checking multiple words at once.

right.text: Drafts 4 words, then verifies all of them in a single GPU step.

left.text: Generates 1 word at a time. Each word requires a full GPU step.

exercises.prompt: What happens to the quality of the AI's output when we use speculative decoding?

options.text: The quality drops slightly but is much faster.
The text becomes random and creative.
The output quality improves beyond the big model's ability.

exercises.explain: Because the big model verifies and corrects all suggestions, the final text is identical to what the big model would have generated on its own.

exercises.prompt: True or False: Verifying multiple tokens at once on a GPU is just as fast as generating a single token.
exercises.explain: Verifying a batch of guessed words takes the same amount of time on a GPU as generating a single word from scratch.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the missing word to explain why verification is so fast on GPUs.
exercises.sentence: GPUs can verify multiple guessed tokens in parallel with no extra time penalty.
exercises.explain: GPUs are designed to process many data points at the exact same time, which is called parallel processing. This is why verifying multiple tokens takes no extra time!

exercises.prompt: Which system is utilizing speculative decoding?

options.text: System A: Generates words one by one slowly.
System B: A tiny model drafts words, and a large model verifies them in parallel.
System C: A large model works alone without any draft helpers.

exercises.explain: Only the system using speculative decoding can guess ahead and verify in parallel to save time. The traditional system must generate each word one by one.

exercises.prompt: Why is speculative decoding considered "free" in terms of GPU time and quality?

options.text: It requires much more GPU memory to run the helper model.
Verifying multiple tokens in parallel takes the same GPU time as generating one token.
It uses a different vocabulary that makes translation faster.
It skips the verification process entirely to save processing power.

exercises.explain: Speculative decoding is a win-win because verifying multiple guessed tokens in parallel takes the same amount of time on a GPU as generating just one token, and the final output remains 100% identical to the big model.
